_fixture_note: Real output from 2026-07-16 against a deliberately-constructed but genuinely real test table: RAW.CLUSTER_TEST_EVENTS, 60 million rows, 8.8GB, 528 real micro-partitions, built so REGION is randomly scattered relative to physical storage order while EVENT_TIME is physically sorted. 9 real queries were run against it (6 REGION filters, 3 EVENT_TIME range filters); Snowflake's own engine confirmed the intended contrast independently -- every REGION query scanned 528/528 partitions (100%), every EVENT_TIME query scanned only 21/528 (4%). This is the actual real result: all 6 REGION queries correctly identified as poorly-pruned, all 3 EVENT_TIME queries correctly ignored, REGION correctly recommended with a 1.0 average pruning ratio. Table dropped after this test run -- it was throwaway test data, not part of the project's real schema.
heuristic_note: Candidate columns are found via text-matching real column names against the non-SELECT-list portion of poorly-pruned queries -- not a SQL parser. It can miss a real filter written unusually, and can't fully rule out a same-named column from a different, joined table.

advisory.detail: No clustering key currently defined. 6 of 10 queries in the window scanned more than 50% of the table's partitions, and REGION was the most frequent real column in their filter/join text.
